text: Create /usr/local/bin/checkuser.sh. The script must accept a username as its first argument. If the user exists, print FOUND. Otherwise, print NOT FOUND to stderr and exit with status 1.
Create /usr/local/bin/listusers.sh. Print the usernames from /etc/passwd, one username per line.
Create /usr/local/bin/loginreport.sh. Display the usernames currently logged in and save the same output to /root/login-report.txt.
Create /usr/local/bin/checkservice.sh. Accept a service name as the first argument. Print ACTIVE if the service is active. Otherwise print INACTIVE and exit with status 1.

Create /usr/local/bin/checkdir.sh. Implement a function named check_directory() that returns success if a supplied directory exists and failure otherwise. Use the function to determine whether /tmp exists and print YES or NO.
